claim: Microchipped dogs and cats are significantly more likely to be returned to owners from a shelter than non-microchipped animals, providing causal evidence for microchip interventions.
stance_target: microchip as a causal driver of shelter return-to-owner outcome
scope_conditions: Hill et al. 2023, JSMCAH; retrospective DAG-adjusted logistic regression; n=5,960 dogs, n=3,489 cats; large municipal shelter in Utah, USA; single-shelter generalizability concern
quoted_support: Dogs and cats with microchips, older animals, healthy animals, neutered animals, and animals brought to the shelter via another public agency were more likely to return to their owners.

claim: Animal sex and season of impound did not affect return-to-owner likelihood for either dogs or cats at a large US municipal shelter.
stance_target: hypothesis that male/intact animals or seasonal timing predict differential RTO rates
scope_conditions: Hill et al. 2023; single municipal Utah shelter; DAG-adjusted logistic regression
quoted_support: Animal sex and season of impound did not affect either dogs' or cats' RTO likelihood.

claim: Healthy condition and older age significantly increase RTO likelihood at shelters, suggesting that younger and sick animals are at highest risk of non-reunion.
stance_target: health status and age as risk stratifiers for shelter RTO outcomes
scope_conditions: Hill et al. 2023; retrospective; single Utah shelter
quoted_support: Older animals, healthy animals [were] more likely to return to their owners.

claim: Animals brought to shelters via another public agency (vs. direct impound) had higher RTO rates, suggesting the intake pathway modulates reunion probability and that stray hold periods should be stratified by source type.
stance_target: impound source type as a confounding variable in lost-dog shelter outcomes
scope_conditions: Hill et al. 2023; Utah shelter; DAG-adjusted model
quoted_support: Animals brought to the shelter via another public agency were more likely to return to their owners... encouraging implementation of new practices, such as modifying stray hold periods based on source type or health status.
